data.title: PBF Energy reports fourth quarter income of $128M, full year loss of $479.5M, declares dividend of $0.275 per share.
data.description: In the fourth quarter, the income from operations stood at $128.0 million, excluding special items, indicating a significant increase as it reached $99.4 million. However, the full-year saw a loss from operations of $54.3 million, which dropped to $479.5 million when special items were taken into account. The company also declared a quarterly dividend during this period.

data.title: Entergy reports 2025 EPS at 51 cents, in top half of guidance range. Initiates 2026 guidance. $ETR
data.description: In the fourth quarter of 2025, Entergy Corporation (NYSE: ETR) announced earnings per share of 51 cents, meeting the top half of its guidance range. This result was reported both on an as-reported and an adjusted (non-GAAP) basis. Throughout the year 2025, the company's earnings per share remained in line with expectations.

data.title: Ridgepost Capital reported Q4 2025 revenue of $81M (still a 15% y/y increase), 15% AUM increase, with full-year revenue at $297.3M.
data.description: Ridgepost Capital, Inc. reported their financial results for Q4 2025 and full year ended December 31, 2025. In Q4, the company saw revenues of $81.0 million, with fee-paying assets under management increasing by 15% year over year to $29.4 billion. GAAP net income was $11.0 million for Q4. For the full fiscal year 2025, revenue was $297.3 million, with fee-paying assets under management remaining at $29.4 billion, a 15% increase year over year. GAAP net income for the full year was $23.0 million. Ridgepost Capital also repurchased approximately 4,348,949 shares in 2025 totaling $47.4 million. A quarterly cash dividend of $0.0375 per share was declared. The company hosted a conference call on February 12, 2026. They have over $43 billion in assets under management and focus on private equity, private credit, and venture capital. Forward-looking statements were made regarding the company's future plans and uncertainties. Ridgepost Capital uses non-GAAP financial measures to provide better comparability of ongoing operating performance to prior periods. For more detailed financial data and reconciliation of non-GAAP financial measures, visit the Benzinga.com website.

data.title: Bombardier reported exceeding 2025 guidance with revenues reaching $9.55 billion, 157 aircraft deliveries, adjusted EBITDA of $1,559 million, and adjusted EPS of $7.72. They also set strong 2026 guidance with expected revenues over $10 billion, adjusted EBITDA above $1,625 million, and free cash flow estimated between $600 million and $1,000 million. Backlog increased to $17.5 billion, showcasing strong demand. Deleveraging efforts showed improvement with adjusted net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio reduced to 1.9x.
data.description: Bombardier Inc. reported strong full-year 2025 revenues of $9.55 billion, exceeding guidance, with 157 aircraft deliveries, an increase of 11 units. Adjusted EBITDA grew 15% year-over-year to $1,559 million, with adjusted EBIT up 26%, reaching $1,108 million. Adjusted net income rose 47% to $805 million, with reported net income up 164% to $975 million. The company's backlog reached $17.5 billion, a $3.1 billion increase. Free cash flow for the year was $1,072 million, with $1,225 million in operating cash flows. The adjusted net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio improved from 2.9x to 1.9x. The company also announced strong 2026 guidance, with expected revenues over $10 billion and adjusted EBITDA greater than $1,625 million. The company aims for continued growth, profitability, and sustainable cash flow. Bombardier's successful turnaround plan since 2021 has set the stage for sustained growth and profitability.

data.title: Agios reported $20.0M in Q4, $54.0M in full-year 2025 revenues. $1.2B cash on Dec 31. Agios focuses on rare diseases.
data.description: Agios Pharmaceuticals, Inc. reported worldwide net revenues of $20.0 million in the fourth quarter and $54.0 million for the full year of 2025. They also announced the availability of AQVESME™ (mitapivat) for thalassemia in the U.S. post-FDA approval. They have $1.2 billion in cash and equivalents. Additionally, they are conducting Phase 2 trials for tebapivat in sickle cell disease and expect results in the second half of 2026. The company's net loss for the fourth quarter of 2025 was $108.0 million. They expect their capital to support the U.S. launch of AQVESME and progression of clinical programs. Further details and financial information are available on Agios Pharmaceuticals' website.

data.title: U.S. stock futures rose after strong January job report, adding 130,000 jobs, with 898,000 job revisions. Dow Jones up 0.21%, S&P 500 up 0.23%. Micron and Novocure in focus.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Thursday after a decline, with major benchmark indices higher following a strong January jobs report: the U.S. economy added 130,000 jobs, surpassing expectations of 70,000 jobs, a significant improvement from December. However, sweeping benchmark revisions erased around 898,000 jobs from payroll estimates. On Wednesday, the Dow Jones index declined from a record-high close on Tuesday. The 10-year Treasury bond yield was at 4.175%, and the two-year bond yield at 3.502%. The Federal Reserve is likely to keep interest rates unchanged in March, with the Dow Jones up 0.21%, S&P 500 up 0.23%, Nasdaq 100 up 0.17%, and Russell 2000 up 0.39%. ETFs SPY and QQQ were up in premarket, with Micron (MU) and Novocure (NVCR) stocks in focus: MU rose 3.33% and NVCR soared nearly 33% in pre-market trading. Full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TransUnion reported strong Q4 2025 results with $1,171M revenue, $101M net income, and $0.52 diluted EPS. For the full year 2025, revenue was $4,576M, net income $455M, and $2.32 diluted EPS. They repurchased $300M of shares and raised dividend to $0.125 per share. They expect 8-9% revenue growth in 2026.
data.description: TransUnion (NYSE: TRU) announced financial results for Q4 2025, with revenue of $1,171 million, a 13% increase from Q4 2024, and a net income of $101 million. They repurchased about $150 million in shares in Q4 and $300 million in 2025. Their quarterly dividend increased to $0.125 per share. For the full year 2025, revenue was $4,576 million, up 9% from 2024, with a net income of $455 million. Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was $1,646 million. They introduced 2026 guidance with an expected revenue growth of 8-9%. In the U.S. Markets segment, Financial Services and Emerging Verticals had revenue growth of 19% and 16%, respectively, in Q4 2025. Adjusted EBITDA was $417 million in Q4. Cash and equivalents were $854 million as of December 31, 2025. The company increased its borrowing capacity to $1.0 billion in February 2026. The outlook for Q1 2026 includes revenue of $1,195-$1,205 million and adjusted EBITDA of $414-$420 million.
